title: A 2,000-Year-Old Computer Found in a Shipwreck
script: They had computers?! In 1901, divers off the Greek island of Antikythera pulled a lump of corroded bronze from a 2,000-year-old shipwreck. For decades it sat ignored. Then X-rays revealed the truth: it held at least 30 interlocking bronze gears, cut by hand. It's now called the Antikythera mechanism — a working analog computer. Turn the crank, and it predicted the positions of the Sun, the Moon, and the planets. It tracked eclipses, and even the 4-year cycle of the ancient Olympic Games. Nothing this complex would appear again for over 1,000 years. We still don't know exactly who built it. So how did the makers of the Antikythera mechanism build a computer the world then forgot for over 1,000 years?
